mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction investment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observations during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 230.0, accelerating inflation forces monetary authorities to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 230.0 are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 50 percent of cases without subsequent rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate profit margins and equity cash flow expectations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 90.0, manufacturing contraction signals supply-side bottlenecks and weakening economic activity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below 90.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of instances.
